planting_details.assumptions_or_notes: Rainfed long-rains season chosen; H614 is a long-season hybrid suitable for the Kenyan highlands around Kitale. Planting window typically mid-March to early April; mid-point date used. Density reflects ~75 cm x 25 cm spacing (~53,000 plants/ha). Row direction unknown; -99 used. Values align with common Kenyan extension recommendations for high-potential maize areas.

events.Assumptions_or_Notes: Basal DAP (18-46-0) at planting, banded just below/alongside the seed (~5 cm). Product rate ~125 kg/ha (common local practice: one 50-kg bag per acre ≈ 125 kg/ha). Nutrients: N = 125 × 0.18 = 22.5 kg/ha; P2O5 = 125 × 0.46 = 57.5 kg/ha; elemental P = 57.5 × 0.4364 ≈ 25.1 kg/ha. No K. Placement reflects hand/spot banding typical of smallholder systems in Trans Nzoia.

Topdress CAN (~26% N) broadcast on the soil surface at ~4 weeks after planting (early vegetative stage). Product rate ≈125 kg/ha to deliver 125 × 0.26 = 32.5 kg N/ha. No P or K supplied. This timing and method (surface broadcast) align with common farmer practice in the area. Total N across events equals exactly 55.0 kg/ha.
